Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additively manufactured heterogeneous precipitation-strengthened high-entropy alloys with high strength and ductility

Composition_Info.Note: Overall AP-HEA composition measured by SEM-EDS; atomic ratio design Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 (at%). Interior FCC region measured composition: Co 49.5, Ni 28.5, Cr 16.3, Fe 1.9, Al 1.7, Ti 2.1 (at%).

Process_Text.original: DED (Directed Energy Deposition) with fiber laser (wavelength 1080 nm, spot diameter 1.2 mm). Layer-by-layer deposition. Powder blends (elemental Co, Ni, Cr, Fe, Al, Ti) with atomic ratio Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 mechanically mixed via 3D mixer. Powders heated to 120°C for 4h for drying before use. Cuboid part fabricated: 60 mm (length) × 15 mm (width) × 6 mm (height). Laser power: 750 W. Scanning speed: 480 mm/min. Layer thickness (lift distance): 0.3 mm. Powder feeding rate: 14.45 g/min. Hatch distance: 0.8 mm. Scan paths shown in XOZ and XOY planes. Argon gas protection.
Process_Text.simplified: DED fabricated HEA with laser power 750 W, scanning speed 480 mm/min, layer thickness 0.3 mm, hatch spacing 0.8 mm, powder feed rate 14.45 g/min under Ar protection.

Process_Info.Equipment: Fiber laser DED system with coaxial powder feeding

Microstructure_Text.original: Typical cellular structure observed in XOY plane. Two separated regions: interior (A) and wall (B). Significant Ti-rich zone in the wall from EDS mapping. High-density dislocations generated in both A and B regions due to repeated compression-tension cycles during laser scanning. Both regions identified as face-centered cubic (FCC) structure by SAED patterns along [110] zone axis. Fine cellular structure with ~5 μm diameter.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Cellular-structured FCC HEA with Ti segregation in cell walls. High dislocation density. Single FCC phase.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; tensile direction vertical to building direction
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8, gauge length 12.5 mm, cross-section 3.2 × 1.5 mm²

Composition_Info.Note: Design atomic ratio Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 (at%). Wall FCC matrix composition: Co 52.2, Ni 23.7, Cr 17.3, Fe 2.7, Al 1.7, Ti 2.4 (at%). Interior FCC region: Co 49.5, Ni 28.5, Cr 16.3, Fe 1.9, Al 1.7, Ti 2.1 (at%). L12 precipitate in wall: Co 20.7, Ni 53.0, Cr 2.1, Fe 0.5, Al 6.1, Ti 17.6 (at%).

Process_Text.original: DED (Directed Energy Deposition) with fiber laser (wavelength 1080 nm, spot diameter 1.2 mm). Layer-by-layer deposition of CoNiCrFeAlTi-based HEA. After DED, isothermally aged at 800°C for 4 h, then cooled to room temperature in air.
Process_Text.simplified: DED fabricated HEA followed by aging at 800°C for 4 h with air cooling.

Process_Info.Equipment: Fiber laser DED system with coaxial powder feeding

Microstructure_Text.original: Heterogeneous cellular structure with distinctly different microstructure: single-phase (FCC) interior and dual-phase (FCC + L12) wall. High-density nanoscale L12 precipitates found in the wall. Interior remains FCC crystal structure (confirmed by SAED). Wall shows L12 nanoprecipitation (dark-field TEM from superlattice reflections). Average L12 precipitate size: 31.0 ± 5.1 nm. Ti element strongly partitions into L12 precipitates while Co, Cr, Fe partition into FCC matrix. APT shows Ni, Al, Ti enriched in L12; Co, Cr, Fe enriched in FCC matrix.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Cellular structure with single-phase FCC interior and dual-phase (FCC + L12) wall. L12 precipitates ~31 nm in wall. Ni, Al, Ti partition to L12; Co, Cr, Fe to FCC.
